ocr.text: The table presents data on serotypes, specifically detailing the number and percentage of each serotype identified. The first
serotype, O6, has a Number of 15 and represents 20.5% of the total. The second serotype, O1, shows a Number of 13,
accounting for 17.8%. The third entry, PA, has a Number of 9, corresponding to a Percentage of 12.3%. Similarly, the serotype
NT/NA also has a Number of 9 and a Percentage of 12.3%. In the fifth row, serotype O11 has a Number of 7, which translates
to 9.6%. The sixth entry, O3, exhibits a Number of 4, making up 5.5%.
This is paralleled by serotype O9, which also has a Number of 4 and a Percentage of 5.5%. Serotype O4 is recorded with a
Number of 3, representing 4.1%, which matches the figures for both serotypes O10 and O15, each with a Number of 3 and a
Percentage of 4.1%. Lastly, serotype O12 shows a Number of 2, corresponding to a Percentage of 2.7%. Overall, the data
reflects the distribution of these serotypes, with serotype O6 being the most prevalent and serotype O12 the least, providing
insight into the relative occurrence of each serotype within the studied population.
